learn.title: 둘레란 무엇인가? — 도형 주변의 거리
learn.content: PERIMETER is the distance AROUND a shape. Imagine an ant walking along the edges of a rectangle. The total distance the ant walks is the perimeter. To find perimeter on a grid, count the unit lengths along the outside of the shape. Example: A rectangle on a grid is 4 units long and 2 units wide. Count: 4 + 2 + 4 + 2 = 12 units. 예: A rectangle on a grid is 3 units long and 1 unit wide. Count the unit lengths around it. What is the perimeter? → 8 units. (3 + 1 + 3 + 1 = 8 units. You add all four sides.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 둘레의 단위 — 길이 단위 (cm/m/in/ft)
learn.content: Perimeter is measured in LINEAR units — centimeters (cm), meters (m), inches (in), feet (ft). NOT square units (those are for area). Think: perimeter = distance = a line around the shape. 예: You measured the perimeter of a desk. Which unit makes sense: 6 square feet or 6 feet? → 6 feet. (Perimeter is a distance, so we use feet (linear unit), not square feet.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 모든 변의 합 — 다각형 둘레 공식
learn.content: You can find the perimeter of ANY polygon by adding up all the side lengths. A triangle with sides 3, 4, and 5: perimeter = 3 + 4 + 5 = 12. A pentagon with sides 2, 2, 3, 3, 4: perimeter = 2 + 2 + 3 + 3 + 4 = 14. Just add every side! 예: A triangle has sides of 6 cm, 8 cm, and 10 cm. What is the perimeter? → 24 cm. (6 + 8 + 10 = 24 cm. Add all the sides.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 격자 위의 둘레 — 단위 길이 세기
learn.content: 격자(grid) 종이에서 둘레: 도형의 가장자리를 따라 작은 단위 칸의 변을 센다.   ⚠️ 점(꼭짓점)이 아니라 변(선분)을 세는 것! 예: 4×3 직사각형 → 4+3+4+3 = 12 단위. 🖍️ 팁: 색연필로 한 변씩 색칠하며 세면 누락 방지.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 직사각형 둘레 — 가로+세로+가로+세로
learn.content: 직사각형은 마주 보는 변이 같음 → 4변 모두 라벨이 없어도 가로 2번 + 세로 2번.   공식: P = 2×(가로 + 세로) = 가로+가로+세로+세로. 예: 가로 6cm, 세로 4cm → P = 2×(6+4) = 20cm. ⚠️ 자주 하는 실수(M02): 라벨된 두 변만 더해서 6+4=10이라 하기. 4변 모두 더해야 함!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 정사각형 둘레 — 한 변 × 4
learn.content: 정사각형은 4변이 모두 같음 → P = 변 × 4 = 변+변+변+변. 예: 한 변 5cm → P = 5×4 = 20cm. ⚠️ 자주 하는 실수(M04): 5+5 = 10 (변 2개만 더함). 4변이 다 같다고 해서 2변만 세면 안 됨! 항상 ×4.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 불규칙 다각형 — 모든 변 빠짐없이
learn.content: L자, T자 모양처럼 변이 많은 도형: 모든 변을 빠짐없이 더해야 함.   방법: 꼭짓점에 1, 2, 3, … 번호 붙이며 한 바퀴 돌기.   변의 개수 = 꼭짓점 개수. 예: L자 (6변, 4+2+2+2+2+4) → P = 16. ⚠️ 자주 하는 실수(M07): 안쪽 굴곡 변 빠뜨리기. 손가락으로 한 번에 외곽 따라가기.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 둘레 vs 넓이 — 절대 헷갈리지 말기 (M01)
learn.content: 📏 둘레(perimeter): 도형 가장자리의 거리 = 변의 합 = 더하기. 단위: cm, m, in, ft.   🟦 넓이(area): 도형이 차지하는 공간 = 가로×세로 = 곱하기. 단위: cm², sq ft. 🐜 비유: 개미가 도형 가장자리를 걸어간 거리 = 둘레. 도형 안을 타일로 채운 개수 = 넓이. 🔍 검산: '안쪽이냐 가장자리냐?' 가장자리 → 더하기 → 둘레.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r2.question: A school had 538 students. 167 students went on a field trip, then 89 returned early. How many students remained on the trip?
practice_r2.explanation: Step 1: 167−89=78 (returned subtracted). Or: trip group = 167, returned 89, still on trip = 167−89 = 78.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Draw two different shapes on a grid that both have a perimeter of 12 units. Describe your shapes.
practice_r3.answer: Example answers: A 4×2 rectangle (4+2+4+2=12) and a 3×3 square (3+3+3+3=12). Or a 5×1 rectangle (5+1+5+1=12). Any two different shapes with perimeter 12.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Jake says the perimeter of a rectangle is 20 cm. The length is 7 cm. What is the width? Explain how you found it.
practice_r3.answer: Width = 3 cm. Two lengths = 7 + 7 = 14. Remaining = 20 − 14 = 6. Two widths = 6, so one width = 3 cm.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Mia says perimeter is measured in square centimeters. Is she correct? Explain why or why not.
practice_r3.answer: Mia is NOT correct. Perimeter is a distance (how far around), so it uses linear units like centimeters, not square centimeters. Square centimeters are for area (the space inside).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Sam says: 'A rectangle 5 cm by 4 cm has perimeter 9 cm because 5+4=9.' Explain Sam's mistake and find the correct perimeter.
practice_r3.answer: Sam은 마주 보는 변(가로 2번, 세로 2번)을 빼먹음. 정확한 둘레 = 5+4+5+4 = 18 cm.
practice_r3.explanation: 직사각형은 마주 보는 변이 같으므로 가로 2개 + 세로 2개 = 4변 모두 더해야 함. 5+4+5+4 = 18 cm.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.1 'Model Perimeter' pp.413-416
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Topic D — Perimeter of Polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
